items.setup: A nervous clerk offers to help — for a cut.

choices.label: Pay him off
choices.flavour: Money buys silence. Usually.
choices.aftermath: The clerk pockets the roll, nods toward a service door, and slides something useful across the counter. Worth every penny, probably.

choices.label: Lean on him
choices.flavour: Some people just need reminding who they're talking to.

roll.aftermathSuccess: He folds fast — and throws in a little extra from under the counter to make you go away.
roll.aftermathFailure: He folds, then panics, then talks to exactly the wrong person. Loudly.

items.setup: A van idles in the alley, keys in the ignition.

choices.label: Hotwire it
choices.flavour: Free transport, if your fingers remember the trick.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Engine purrs on the first splice. Anyone tracking your movements just lost the trail.
roll.aftermathFailure: The alarm you didn't see goes off. The whole block now knows the van exists, and roughly where you're standing.

choices.label: Leave it
choices.flavour: Free lunches are how they catch you.
choices.aftermath: You walk past. The van idles on, keeping its secrets. Probably for the best.

items.setup: A guard dog dozes across the corridor.

choices.label: Tiptoe past
choices.flavour: Dogs sleep light. So should you.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Not a whisker twitches. The corridor's yours, and quieter than ever.
roll.aftermathFailure: One floorboard. One bark. The whole building hears the second one.

choices.label: Toss it your lunch
choices.flavour: An old trick. Dogs respect catering.
choices.aftermath: The dog dines well — on the sandwich and the bribe money tucked under it. Cash gone, corridor peaceful, tail wagging.

items.setup: The cellar's open, full of vintages.

choices.label: Pocket a bottle
choices.flavour: One bottle. Who counts bottles?
choices.aftermath: The '82 sells itself. So does the empty slot in the rack — somebody is absolutely going to notice that gap.

choices.label: Stay disciplined
choices.flavour: Professionals don't drink on the job.
choices.aftermath: You close the cellar door gently behind you. The job stays tidy, and so do your heads.

items.setup: The building's electrics, exposed.

choices.label: Cut the power
choices.flavour: Dark buildings hide a multitude of sins.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Half the floor goes dark, cameras included. You've never looked better — mostly because nobody can see you.
roll.aftermathFailure: Sparks, a pop, and every backup light in the place snaps on at once. Subtle.

choices.label: Leave it
choices.flavour: Live wires bite people who improvise.
choices.aftermath: You leave the electrics to the electricians. The hum follows you down the hall, unbothered.

items.setup: A former crewmate is casing the same joint.

choices.label: Team up
choices.flavour: Two crews, one safe. What's a little history between thieves?
choices.aftermath: Old habits sync up fast and the haul gets heavier — but two crews make twice the footprints.

choices.label: Send them off with a tip
choices.flavour: Send them somewhere nicer. Far away.
choices.aftermath: You buy their exit with a fat tip; they leave you a parting gift from the old days. Fair trade, mostly.

items.setup: A smaller safe in the corner, not on the plan.

choices.label: Crack it open
choices.flavour: Safes that aren't on the plan are somebody's secret.

roll.aftermathSuccess: It gives on the third try — somebody's rainy-day fund, now relocating to a drier climate.
roll.aftermathFailure: It holds, and the drill's whine carries further than you'd like. Time to not be near this corner.

choices.label: Leave it — stay on task
choices.flavour: The plan exists for a reason.
choices.aftermath: You let it keep its secret and stay on schedule. Discipline pays in quiet, which is my favourite currency.

items.setup: Real janitors, working late.

choices.label: Grab a uniform
choices.flavour: Nobody looks twice at a mop.
choices.aftermath: One overall, lightly used, lifted off the trolley. Whoever wears it suddenly belongs here.

choices.label: Hide and wait
choices.flavour: Patience is free. Mostly.
choices.aftermath: Twenty minutes behind a shelf of solvents, and the floor empties out on its own. Worth the cramp.

items.setup: A wallet on the floor.

choices.label: Take the cash
choices.flavour: Finders, keepers. That's the law, roughly.
choices.aftermath: Thicker than it looked. Whoever dropped it was having a very good night — was.

choices.label: Wipe it and leave it
choices.flavour: Touch nothing you don't need.
choices.aftermath: Wiped clean and left where it fell. No prints, no story, no loose thread with your name on it.

items.setup: A police frequency, crackling.

choices.label: Listen in
choices.flavour: Know what they know. If the static lets you.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Through the crackle you catch their patrol chatter — you now know exactly what's waiting in the next room.
roll.aftermathFailure: You key the wrong channel and broadcast two seconds of breathing to every cop in the precinct.

choices.label: Keep moving
choices.flavour: Radios cut both ways.
choices.aftermath: You let the static talk to itself and keep walking. Some conversations aren't yours to join.

items.setup: Something's stashed beneath a loose floorboard.

choices.label: Pry it up
choices.flavour: People hide things under floors for a reason.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Up it comes — and underneath, a strongbox somebody really didn't want found. Heavy strongbox.
roll.aftermathFailure: The board cracks like a gunshot. Nothing underneath, either. Insult, meet injury.

choices.label: Leave it
choices.flavour: Not every creak is treasure.
choices.aftermath: You step over it and onward. Whatever's down there can stay somebody else's secret.

items.setup: A crew member wants out.

choices.label: Talk them round
choices.flavour: Everyone wobbles. Good crews catch each other.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Five quiet words and they're back in — sharper than before, and they dig out their lucky kit to prove it.
roll.aftermathFailure: The pep talk turns into an argument, and arguments echo in empty buildings.

choices.label: Cut them loose
choices.flavour: A clean break beats a shaky hand.
choices.aftermath: They take their cut and the back stairs. Smaller share, calmer room — nobody freezes at the wrong moment now.

items.setup: The mark's car turns into the drive.

choices.label: Slip out the back
choices.flavour: Back doors exist for moments like this.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Out and gone before his engine stops ticking. He'll never know how close he came.
roll.aftermathFailure: The back gate squeals like it's paid to. Lights come on behind you.

choices.label: Play it cool as staff
choices.flavour: Confidence is a uniform.

roll.aftermathSuccess: A nod, a 'good evening, sir', and he hands you his coat. You're staff now. Carry on.
roll.aftermathFailure: 'I don't have staff tonight,' he says, reaching for his phone. Move.

items.setup: A workman's tool bag, abandoned.

choices.label: Grab it
choices.flavour: Tools don't ask whose hands they're in.
choices.aftermath: Good kit, barely used. Somebody's loss is your trade upgrade — distribute accordingly.

choices.label: Leave it — might be watched
choices.flavour: Bait looks exactly like luck.
choices.aftermath: You give it a wide berth. If it was a trap it's still waiting; if it wasn't, no harm done. Quiet either way.

items.setup: An officer offers to look the other way.

choices.label: Bribe him
choices.flavour: Expensive friends are still friends.
choices.aftermath: The envelope vanishes into his jacket, and so does tonight's report. Money well buried.

choices.label: Refuse
choices.flavour: Owe a cop nothing, ever.
choices.aftermath: He shrugs and strolls off. No favour, no leash. You keep your money and your independence.

items.setup: Priceless canvases line the hall.

choices.label: Cut one out
choices.flavour: Canvas rolls up small. Alarms don't.

roll.aftermathSuccess: The blade whispers around the frame and the old master rolls up like a newspaper. Museum-quality work, in every sense.
roll.aftermathFailure: The frame was wired. Every bell in the building agrees with it, emphatically.

choices.label: Photograph them for the fence
choices.flavour: Sometimes the picture of the picture pays.
choices.aftermath: Twelve frames, no flash, and the fence already has a buyer who pays for knowing what hangs where. Easy money.

items.setup: The security footage, yours to wipe.

choices.label: Wipe the footage
choices.flavour: Tonight never happened. If you type fast enough.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Eight hours of footage, gone like it was never filmed. As far as the record shows, you're a rumour.
roll.aftermathFailure: The system locks you out and flags the attempt. Now there's footage of you trying to delete the footage.

choices.label: Leave it
choices.flavour: Touch the servers, leave a fingerprint somewhere in them.
choices.aftermath: You leave the blinking racks alone. The tapes keep what they already have — no more, no less.

items.setup: You could stage a commotion across the floor.

choices.label: Pull it off
choices.flavour: All eyes one way, all hands the other.

roll.aftermathSuccess: The commotion blooms beautifully — every eye goes one way while the goods drift the other. Textbook.
roll.aftermathFailure: The 'fainting guest' overacts. Now there's a crowd, a first-aider, and far too much attention.

choices.label: Don't risk it
choices.flavour: The quietest move is no move.
choices.aftermath: You let the floor stay boring. Boring has never once called the police.

items.setup: A sealed case, clearly valuable, tamper-locked. It's a bet on how long you'll stay.

choices.label: Take it along
choices.flavour: Heavy, locked, and humming with promise.
choices.aftermath: It's cuffed to your evening now — conspicuous, awkward, and worth nothing until you've carried it far enough to crack.

choices.label: Leave it
choices.flavour: Some bets you don't carry on your back.
choices.aftermath: You leave the mystery on its shelf. Whatever's inside, it isn't slowing you down tonight.

items.setup: A tray of gems scatters across the marble.

choices.label: Scramble for the lot
choices.flavour: Fast hands, full pockets.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Every last stone, swept up before they stop bouncing. The marble's clean and you're carrying a jeweller's window.
roll.aftermathFailure: You come up with a decent fistful — and kick the tray across the floor doing it. That clatter had an audience.

choices.label: Grab a handful
choices.flavour: Greed trips. Take what's nearest.
choices.aftermath: A calm handful of the closest stones and you're moving again. The rest can glitter for whoever finds them.

items.setup: One guard who can't be bought.

choices.label: Overpower him
choices.flavour: Some conversations are shorter than others.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Quick, quiet, and he's napping in a supply closet with his radio off. One less pair of eyes on the rounds.
roll.aftermathFailure: He's stronger than he looks and twice as loud. The scuffle rattles down three corridors.

choices.label: Slip past unseen
choices.flavour: He can't refuse what he never sees.

roll.aftermathSuccess: He stares dutifully at nothing while you ghost by behind him. Integrity intact, route cleared.
roll.aftermathFailure: He turns at exactly the wrong second. Honest and observant — the worst combination.

items.setup: Building plans on a desk.

choices.label: Study them
choices.flavour: Knowledge weighs nothing. Time doesn't.
choices.aftermath: Three minutes hunched over the plans — you know what the next room holds now, but the clock noticed you standing still.

choices.label: Pocket and sell them
choices.flavour: Paper's worth more to the right buyer.
choices.aftermath: Rolled, tubed, and pre-sold. The fence loves architecture — somebody's security budget just became your payday.

items.setup: A leather-topped desk and a wall safe behind a portrait.

choices.label: Crack the wall safe
choices.flavour: Behind every portrait, an ego. Behind some, a safe.

roll.aftermathSuccess: The dial gives up its little secret and the safe coughs out the good stuff. The portrait looks on, scandalised.
roll.aftermathFailure: Three wrong combinations and a soft electronic chirp you really didn't want to hear.

choices.label: Pocket the desk cash
choices.flavour: The obvious drawer, the obvious prize.
choices.aftermath: Top drawer, cash clip, done. Some men keep their walking-around money exactly where a professional would look first.

items.setup: Unaccounted cash, found mid-job.

choices.label: Share it evenly
choices.flavour: Even cuts keep crews together.
choices.aftermath: Everyone gets a slice, plus a little kit from the same stash — funny how fair maths makes a crew sharper.

choices.label: Pocket it quietly
choices.flavour: What they don't know can't be deducted.
choices.aftermath: Into one jacket it goes, cash and a clever little extra — and a sideways glance from someone who maybe noticed. That travels.

items.setup: Someone clocks a crew member's face.

choices.label: Pay for silence
choices.flavour: Memory has a market rate.
choices.aftermath: The roll changes hands and the memory develops a convenient blur. Faces? What faces.

choices.label: Lean on them
choices.flavour: A quiet word, properly delivered.

roll.aftermathSuccess: You explain, gently, what forgetting is worth. They forget. Everyone walks away unbruised.
roll.aftermathFailure: They nod, wide-eyed — then make a phone call the moment you turn your back.

items.setup: Trophies, a humidor, a personal safe behind the portrait.

choices.label: Go for the safe
choices.flavour: Personal safes hold personal favourites.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Click. No cash inside — something rarer. The mark's private toy just became crew equipment.
roll.aftermathFailure: It's a better safe than he is a man. It holds, and the attempt leaves scratches somebody will read.

choices.label: Pocket a cigar and go
choices.flavour: Take the small luxury and keep walking.
choices.aftermath: One cigar for morale, one jade ashtray for the fence. In and out before the leather chair stops creaking.

items.setup: A wire glints across the doorway.

choices.label: Step over it
choices.flavour: High knees, held breath.

roll.aftermathSuccess: One long stride apiece and you're over. The wire keeps its tension and its silence.
roll.aftermathFailure: A trailing bootlace. The wire sings, and somewhere a panel lights up.

choices.label: Disarm it
choices.flavour: Cut the right end of the right wire.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Snipped at the anchor, coiled, pocketed. The doorway's safe for everyone behind you, too.
roll.aftermathFailure: Wrong end. The mechanism does exactly what it was built to do, loudly.

items.setup: The ballroom's hosting a black-tie party — crowds everywhere.

choices.label: Work the room
choices.flavour: Crowds are camouflage with cocktails.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Two handshakes, one dance, and a donation envelope finds a new charity: yours.
roll.aftermathFailure: Wrong name, wrong anecdote, and a hostess whose smile drops half an inch. You're being watched now.

choices.label: Skirt the edges
choices.flavour: Be the waiter nobody remembers.
choices.aftermath: You hug the walls with a tray and a bland expression. Three hundred guests, zero memories of you.

items.setup: A guard greets a crew member like an old colleague.

choices.label: Chat them up
choices.flavour: Old colleagues love to talk shop.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Five minutes of 'remember when' and he's waved you through — plus told you exactly what's around the next corner.
roll.aftermathFailure: The small talk hits a wrong detail. His smile stays; his hand drifts toward the radio.

choices.label: Avoid them
choices.flavour: Familiar faces have long memories.
choices.aftermath: A sharp turn down a side corridor, and the reunion never happens. Some friendships are best left unconfirmed.

items.setup: A tiny service hatch between floors.

choices.label: Send your smallest through
choices.flavour: Small spaces, big shortcuts.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Up the shaft, through the pantry, back with the silver. The hatch fits one thief and one very full bag.
roll.aftermathFailure: Halfway up, the pulley slips — the crash rattles two floors and the shortcut becomes a spotlight.

choices.label: Leave it
choices.flavour: Not everyone fits. Not everything's worth it.
choices.aftermath: You close the hatch on the idea. The stairs are slower, but nobody gets stuck in a wall tonight.

items.setup: Cash that looks a little too crisp.

choices.label: Grab a bundle
choices.flavour: Crisp bills, suspicious shine.

roll.aftermathSuccess: You skim the genuine notes off the top of the funny money. The fence can sort the rest from the rest.
roll.aftermathFailure: Closer up, it's all printer's art — pretty, worthless, not worth the bag space. You set it back down.

choices.label: Leave it
choices.flavour: Fake money buys real trouble.
choices.aftermath: You leave the counterfeit where it lies. Passing fakes is somebody else's sentence — your night stays clean.

items.setup: A staffer offers a quiet tour for a tip.

choices.label: Tip and follow
choices.flavour: Guides know where the bodies — sorry, bottles — are.
choices.aftermath: The tip stings, but the steward talks all the way down the stairs — you know exactly what the next room is hiding.

choices.label: Decline
choices.flavour: Free tours come with witnesses.
choices.aftermath: A polite no, and the steward melts back into the service corridor. You keep your money and your anonymity.

items.setup: The floor ahead looks rigged.

choices.label: Pick across it
choices.flavour: Light feet, lighter conscience.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Heel, toe, breathe. You read the floor like sheet music and arrive on the far side unannounced.
roll.aftermathFailure: One tile sinks half an inch. Somewhere below, something begins to whir.

choices.label: Wedge and disarm it
choices.flavour: Beat the floor at its own game.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Wedged, jumpered, dead. The floor's just a floor now — for you and everyone coming after.
roll.aftermathFailure: The wedge slips mid-job, and the plate registers exactly the kind of visitor it was built for.

items.setup: It crackles — someone wants a status check.

choices.label: Answer it
choices.flavour: Be the voice they expect to hear.

roll.aftermathSuccess: 'All quiet on three.' Beautifully bored delivery — they log it and move on. You just bought the whole floor some calm.
roll.aftermathFailure: 'Who is this?' is not the response you wanted. The line goes dead in a way that means someone's coming.

choices.label: Ignore it
choices.flavour: Silence is an answer too.
choices.aftermath: You let it crackle into nothing. Maybe they'll assume the unit's broken. Units break all the time.

items.setup: A wall of small locked drawers, each maybe holding something.

choices.label: Force a few open
choices.flavour: Little locks guard nice things.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Three drawers, two disappointments, one velvet tray of very portable value. The pry bar earns its keep.
roll.aftermathFailure: The drawers are sturdier than they look, and prying them sounds like a woodshop. Nothing to show for the noise.

choices.label: Take the one left ajar
choices.flavour: Take what's offered.
choices.aftermath: The open drawer holds the collector's favourite — which is exactly why it was nearest to hand. Lifted, pocketed, gone.

items.setup: A staffer offers evidence on the mark — for cover.

choices.label: Take it
choices.flavour: Evidence cuts whoever holds it.
choices.aftermath: She hands over the folder and a little insurance to go with it — but a deal struck in a stairwell never stays whisper-quiet.

choices.label: Stay out of it
choices.flavour: Other people's wars stay other people's.
choices.aftermath: You wish her luck and keep your distance. Her crusade, her consequences — your night stays simple.

items.setup: A jewel under thick glass.

choices.label: Cut the glass
choices.flavour: Thick glass rewards a steady hand.

roll.aftermathSuccess: The circle of glass lifts away like a lid and the jewel drops into a gloved palm. Surgery, basically.
roll.aftermathFailure: The pane crazes, then shatters — and a case like that doesn't break quietly. Sirens are a genre of music now.

choices.label: Swap it for a fake
choices.flavour: If the fake is good, no one ever looks.

roll.aftermathSuccess: The replica settles onto the plinth like it grew there. They'll guard the fake for years while the real one travels with you.
roll.aftermathFailure: The fake catches the light all wrong, and you abort mid-reach with a guard inbound.

items.setup: An abandoned crew's kit bag.

choices.label: Split it among the crew
choices.flavour: Share the wealth, share the edge.
choices.aftermath: The bag opens like a small Christmas. Everyone walks away a little better equipped than the crew that abandoned it.

choices.label: Keep it whole for one specialist
choices.flavour: One perfect kit beats four half-measures.
choices.aftermath: The whole rig goes to your specialist, who assembles it with frankly worrying glee. Quality over equality tonight.

items.setup: A resident peers from a doorway.

choices.label: Reassure them
choices.flavour: A calm voice closes more doors than a crowbar.

roll.aftermathSuccess: 'Building maintenance, ma'am. Gas check.' She nods, satisfied, and the door clicks shut on the whole question.
roll.aftermathFailure: She doesn't buy a word of it, and her hallway light stays on. So does her phone screen.

choices.label: Duck away
choices.flavour: What they can't describe, they can't report.
choices.aftermath: Around the corner before her eyes adjust. She saw a shadow, and shadows don't make it into statements.

items.setup: A ring of keys on a hook.

choices.label: Pocket them
choices.flavour: Every key is a door you haven't met yet.
choices.aftermath: The ring's in your pocket and the next room's lock is no mystery now — but an empty hook gets noticed fast.

choices.label: Copy and replace
choices.flavour: Borrow, mould, return. Nobody's the wiser.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Pressed in putty and back on the hook in ninety seconds. You know what the next door wants, and nobody's missing a thing.
roll.aftermathFailure: The mould smears and the keys jangle back onto the hook crooked. Somebody heard that, and somebody tidy will notice.

items.setup: A painting swings aside to reveal a recess.

choices.label: Reach in
choices.flavour: Recesses hold what shelves can't.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Elbow-deep, and out comes the strongbox — small, dense, and very much the point of having a secret wall.
roll.aftermathFailure: A sleeve catches the frame and the painting swings down off its hinge. Empty recess, loud wall.

choices.label: Note it and leave
choices.flavour: Mark it, mention it, monetise it.
choices.aftermath: You sketch the spot and lift the small stuff within reach. The fence pays for maps like this — buyers love a treasure hunt.

items.setup: Your driver radios a warning about the route.

choices.label: Reroute on their word
choices.flavour: The van sees what you can't.
choices.aftermath: You take the long way on my say-so, and the long way is beautifully empty. You're welcome, by the way.

choices.label: Stick to the plan
choices.flavour: Plans are plans for a reason.

roll.aftermathSuccess: Fine, ignore the driver — you thread the original route clean anyway. I'd be annoyed if I weren't impressed.
roll.aftermathFailure: And there's the patrol I mentioned. Next time the van speaks, the van is right.

items.setup: A book of where the real valuables sit.

choices.label: Study it
choices.flavour: Read the house's own homework.
choices.aftermath: The ledger reads like a guided tour — you know exactly what's sitting in the next room, and in whose name.

choices.label: Tear out a page and run
choices.flavour: One page, torn fast, sells faster.
choices.aftermath: Rrrip. One page of names and numbers — the kind certain buyers pay handsomely never to see published.

items.setup: The crew's getting cocky mid-job.

choices.label: Indulge a moment
choices.flavour: Morale is equipment too.
choices.aftermath: One glass each, one toast to the crew — spirits lift and hands steady. The laughter carried further than you think, though.

choices.label: Keep focused
choices.flavour: Celebrate in the van, not the vault.
choices.aftermath: Corks stay in. The crew swallows the grin and gets back to work — save the toast for when I'm driving you out of here.
